approvalBoundary: Approves the current simulator-backed web intake baseline only. It does not approve live NHS login, telephony, live provider onboarding, or production assurance signoff.

mandatorySuites.label: Red flag decision table and malicious upload suite
mandatorySuites.summary: Rule-row assertions and malicious upload fixtures preserve urgent-required versus issued separation and quarantine-first evidence handling.

mandatorySuites.label: Duplicate submit refresh replay and collision review suite
mandatorySuites.summary: Replay and collision proof keeps duplicate requests notifications and mutable post-promotion reopen at zero.

mandatorySuites.label: End-to-end channel and accessibility regression suite
mandatorySuites.summary: All four request-type journeys and accessibility assertions preserve same-shell continuity keyboard focus sticky controls and reduced-motion semantics.

mandatorySuites.label: Submission burst performance and resilience suite
mandatorySuites.summary: Burst and replay pressure preserve exact-once side effects and machine-readable degraded-mode browser budgets.

gateQuestions.question: Are tasks 139-168 complete source-traceable and internally coherent?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The checklist marks 139-168 complete and each conformance row binds source references implementation evidence and automated proof artifacts.

gateQuestions.question: Did mandatory suites 165-168 pass with machine-readable evidence?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The gate binds directly to the 165 red-flag and upload results, 166 exact-once replay results, 167 channel accessibility results, and 168 resilience results.

gateQuestions.question: Are the canonical Phase 1 invariants demonstrably true?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Promotion, replay, safety, triage, receipt, notification, browser continuity, and resilience invariants are represented as explicit booleans and conformance rows.

gateQuestions.question: Are simulator-first assumptions explicit and bounded?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The verdict approves only the simulator-first web intake baseline and excludes live auth, telephony, live provider, and production signoff claims.

gateQuestions.question: Which items are deferred to Phase 2 or later and why are they not blockers?
gateQuestions.answer: They are published as structured deferred_non_blocking items with scope, rationale, owner phase, and source references.

gateQuestions.question: Is there any unresolved contradiction between the blueprint corpus prompt contracts implemented artifacts and verification results?
gateQuestions.answer: No unresolved contradiction is identified for the simulator-first Phase 1 baseline. Later live-provider work is deferred without weakening the Phase 1 correctness rules.

contradictionChecks.summary: The approved verdict is scoped to simulator-backed web intake and does not claim production live-provider readiness.
Seq 166 and seq 168 both keep duplicate authoritative side effects at zero.
Browser continuity accessibility reduced motion and degraded-mode proof are first-class conformance rows and evidence artifacts.
